text: Hi guys, this is Dr. Andrew Iranihov from Surgical Oasis Institute. Today I want to answer this question, what is internal hernia?
The video shows a segment of intestine trapped within a defect in the abdominal wall, caused by previous scar tissue, indicative of an internal hernia.
That's very different with the abdominal wall hernia defect.

Using specialized laparoscopic instruments, the surgeon gently removes the incarcerated loop of the small intestine from a visible hole created by scar tissue in the right lower quadrant, resulting from a previous appendectomy.
The internal hernia is different from the abdominal wall hernia but can have the same potential of incarceration and bowel obstruction and therefore required to have surgical treatment.

At Surgical Oasis Institute I have expertise to repair different type of hernias whether abdominal wall or internal hernia so therefore please contact my office at nine four nine six four six eight four four for more information. Thank you.
